inboxRoot.description: Root of the agent-bridge inbox (per-target subdirs live under `<inboxRoot>/openclaw/<target>/`). Defaults to ~/.agent-bridge/inbox.

inboxDir.description: Legacy alias for `inboxRoot` (pre-2.1.0). Still honoured for upgrade compatibility.

keysDir.description: Directory holding SSH keys for SFTP outbound replies. Defaults to ~/.agent-bridge/keys.

pollIntervalMs.description: Polling interval for inbox watcher. Default: 2000.

machineName.description: Local machine name used as the 'from' field on outbound BridgeMessages. Defaults to hostname.

agentId.description: OpenClaw agent id to target when building session keys. Defaults to 'main'.

peer_id.description: Plugin-level default peer id (e.g. Ethan's Telegram user id '6164541473'). Used as a fallback when a target's own peer_id is missing OR when auto-discovery can't resolve one from the OpenClaw config / allowFrom list. Optional.

replyVia.description: Plugin-level default reply routing for inbound bridge messages. 'telegram' routes the agent's reply back through the same Telegram chat that injected the session (visible to Ethan's phone). 'agent-bridge' routes the reply back to the SENDER machine via agent-bridge SFTP delivery (invisible to Telegram). If unset, routing mirrors the sender: messages with fromTarget reply over agent-bridge; messages without it reply through Telegram. Per-target overrides win over this default.

targets.description: OPTIONAL: Map of target-name -> routing config. If present, acts as an advanced override — each target name becomes an inbox subdir at `<inboxRoot>/openclaw/<name>/` and identifies which Telegram account/session to inject bridge messages into. If absent or empty (the default), the plugin AUTO-DISCOVERS one target per entry in `channels.telegram.accounts` on the OpenClaw global config. Peer id resolution: explicit `targets.<name>.peer_id` -> `channels.agent-bridge.config.peer_id` -> `meta.user_id` on the global config -> first chat id in `channels.telegram.accounts[<name>].allowFrom`.

openclaw_channel.description: Which OpenClaw channel hosts the target session. Typically 'telegram'.

account.description: Account id within the chosen channel (e.g. 'default', 'clawdiboi2').

peer_id.description: Stable peer id of the direct chat to inject into (e.g. Ethan's Telegram user id '6164541473'). Optional when a plugin-level `peer_id` is configured.

agent_id.description: Optional per-target override of the top-level `agentId`.

replyVia.description: Override plugin-level `replyVia` for this target. 'telegram' = reply goes out through Telegram (Ethan's phone pings). 'agent-bridge' = reply is SFTP-delivered back to the sender machine as a BridgeMessage (no Telegram traffic). Default: inherits plugin-level, then mirrors the sender (agent-bridge when fromTarget is present, otherwise telegram).

fanOutChannel.description: Deprecated (v2.0.x) — prefer registering the target session directly via `targets.<name>`. Kept for legacy compatibility.

agent-bridge.label: Agent Bridge
agent-bridge.description: Routes Agent Bridge messages into OpenClaw sessions and sends replies back over Telegram or Agent Bridge.
